pmos /magazine windowing + prep snapshot-scoping — implementation plan (260624-xck)
Created using pmos‑learnkit

/magazine windowing + prep snapshot-scoping — implementation plan (260624-xck)

Story: 260624-xck · Epic: 260624-2bm · Route: skill · Tier: 2 · Plugin: pmos-learnkit / magazine

Spec (design_doc): ../../02_design.html — Decisions D1/D2/D6/D7, Inv-1..Inv-5, FR-1.x/2.x/6.x, Risks. Task status lives only in tasks.yaml (D4); this body never holds status.

Done when: bash tests/structure.test.sh exits 0 (every bundled --selftest green, incl. magazine-run.js + fetch-feed.js + magazine-watch.js); interactive discover requires a window (bare call exits non-zero), resolves lower+upper bounds, rejects unknown flags loudly; prep crawls only the single latest snapshot; the cursor is no longer read on the interactive path while watch's enqueue/drain contract stays green; SKILL.md + pipeline.md describe the real flag surface.

Code Study Notes

Patterns to follow

  • scripts/magazine-run.js:423-442 — parseOpts(argv): a flat for loop of else if (a === '--flag') arms over a shared opts bag used by every verb (discover/prep/status/enqueue/drain). There is no else branch → an unrecognized flag is silently dropped (the root cause of finding #1). New flags are added as more arms here.
  • scripts/fetch-feed.js:87-91 — windowItems(items, sinceISO, max): filters it.published > sinceISO (lower bound only), sorts desc, caps at max. The single home of the crawl-time date filter — the natural hook for the upper bound.
  • scripts/magazine-run.js:94-103 — fetchOne(feed, since, max): shells fetch-feed.js with --since/--max; the per-feed bridge that must also forward --until.
  • scripts/magazine-state.js:82-96 — load() returns {cursors:{}, items:{}} on any read failure (absence-tolerant — Inv-4); save() is atomic temp-then-rename. A new top-level snapshot key rides this unchanged.
  • scripts/magazine-state.js:104-117 — discover() is idempotent: if (state.items[guid]) return false (D6/Inv-2 — re-discovering an already-advanced item is a no-op, preserving its status).

Existing code to reuse

  • magazine-state.js load/save — snapshot persistence reuses them; no new IO path.
  • magazine-run.js:42-44 safeGuid — already exported; not changed here (Story B owns its reuse).
  • fetch-feed.js arg('--flag', default) helper (:152-156) — the --until arg is parsed the same way as --since.
  • In-file --selftest harnesses in both scripts (hermetic, file:// fixtures under tests/fixtures/) — every new branch gets assertions here; tests/structure.test.sh runs them all.

Constraints discovered

  • Shared parseOpts (Inv-2): loud-reject must allow flags legitimately used by any verb. Audited at grill: the only flags passed by existing callers (incl. the watch wrapper's enqueue/drain --max --root) are --feed/--feeds/--since/--max/--root/--min-chars/--backfill/--budget-sec — all already known. Register the four window flags + --snapshot in the same bag and reject only unknown ---prefixed tokens.
  • Cursor is watch-load-bearing (D7): the scheduled enqueue/drain pipeline (magazine-watch.js, cmdEnqueue's forward-only per-feed cursor, FR-7/FR-R4) is the only cursor reader that must survive. Do not touch enqueue/drain. watch.test.sh is the regression guard.
  • Snapshot is single-latest (D2): exactly one state.snapshot = {id, guids}, overwritten each discover. No list, no GC. --snapshot <id> parses to a field but selects nothing (inert hook).
  • Printed JSON unchanged: discover still prints the snapshot array to stdout (main dispatch :664); the new persisted state.snapshot is internal.
  • Bare-date vs ISO: --from/--to take YYYY-MM-DD (inclusive day endpoints); --since/--until take full ISO. --to 2026-03-31 means "through end of 2026-03-31" → upper bound is 2026-03-31T23:59:59.999Z.

Stack signals

  • No host manifest at the magazine skill dir — pure zero-dep Node ≥18 (global fetch), no package.json, no npm (Inv-3). Reference system: the existing magazine scripts themselves. Tests are bash (tests/*.test.sh) + in-file node script --selftest.

Decision Log

#DecisionOptions consideredRationale
P1Interactive Phase-6 render stops advancing the per-feed cursor; only watch's enqueue advances it (resolves the AC8 open question).(a) interactive render keeps advancing the cursor (vestigial but harmless); (b) interactive render stops touching it.(b). D7 makes the cursor watch-internal; interactive discover no longer reads it, so advancing it on the interactive path is dead state that could confuse a future reader. Stop advancing it interactively (remove the Phase-6 instruction + any interactive advanceCursors call); enqueue keeps advancing its own forward-only cursor for the scheduler. Article-feed cursors become vestigial-but-harmless (no interactive reader, no article watch).
P2Window resolution + the require-a-window guard live in cmdDiscover, not parseOpts.(a) resolve/guard in parseOpts; (b) parseOpts only parses, cmdDiscover resolves bounds + enforces "window required".(b). parseOpts is shared by all verbs — enqueue/drain legitimately take no window. Keep parseOpts a dumb parser (+loud reject); put the discover-only "must have a window" rule and the precedence ladder in cmdDiscover so other verbs are unaffected.
P3Upper bound is applied in fetch-feed.js windowItems (crawl-time), threaded via fetchOne.(a) filter in cmdDiscover after fetch; (b) extend windowItems.(b). The lower bound already lives there (:89); colocating both bounds keeps one filter, one selftest surface, and matches FR-6.1's "same call-site" intent. cmdDiscover stays a coordinator.

Tasks

T1: Window resolution + require-a-window + loud unknown-flag reject (tracer bullet)

Slice shape: vertical — proves the new required-window contract end-to-end (parse → resolve → run/error) for the dullest case (--days N), exercising the riskiest unproven point: erroring on bare/unknown input where today it silently succeeds.

Spec refs: FR-1.1, FR-1.2, FR-1.3, D1, D7 · ACs: AC1 (lower bound + precedence), AC3 (loud reject), AC4 (bare errors).

  1. RED. In scripts/magazine-run.js selftest(), add assertions: (a) parseOpts(['--bogus','x']) path → cmdDiscover wrapper throws / the dispatch exits non-zero naming --bogus; (b) resolving a window from {days:7} yields a lower bound ≈ now − 7d ISO and no upper bound; (c) resolving from {} (bare) throws a "window required" error. Run node scripts/magazine-run.js --selftest → observe the new assertions FAIL.
  2. GREEN — parseOpts. Add arms to parseOpts (:423): --days (int), --until (str), --from (str), --to (str), --snapshot (str, stored only). Add a terminal else if (a.startsWith('--')) { process.stderr.write('magazine-run: unknown flag '+a+'\n'); process.exit(64); }. Keep all existing arms verbatim.
  3. GREEN — resolveWindow. Add a pure exported helper resolveWindow(opts, nowISO) returning {since, until}: lower-bound precedence --from/--since > --days (now − N·86400·1000 ms, ISO) ; no cursor / no unbounded fallback. If none of --from/--since/--days/--to/--until is present → throw new Error('discover: a window is required — pass --from/--to, --since/--until, or --days N'). Upper bound from --to/--until (T2 wires the filter; here just resolve it).
  4. GREEN — wire cmdDiscover. At the top of cmdDiscover (:108) call const {since, until} = resolveWindow(opts, new Date().toISOString()) and pass since to fetchOne (until wired in T2). Remove the implicit opts.since-or-null pass-through that allowed an unbounded crawl.
  5. Inline verification. node scripts/magazine-run.js --selftest → PASS. Behavioral proof-of-life: node scripts/magazine-run.js discover 2>&1; echo "exit=$?" with no flags → prints the "window required" message, exit=64. node scripts/magazine-run.js discover --bogus 1 2>&1; echo "exit=$?" → names --bogus, exit=64. node scripts/magazine-run.js discover --feed tests/fixtures/<feed>.xml --days 3650 → exits 0, prints a snapshot array (fixture items are old, so a wide window returns them).

Depends on: none.

T2: Upper-bound filter in fetch-feed.js (range crawl)

Slice shape: vertical — a date-range crawl returns only in-range items end-to-end.

Spec refs: FR-6.1, FR-6.2 · ACs: AC2.

  1. RED. In fetch-feed.js selftest(), add: against the local fixture, windowItems(items, sinceISO, max, untilISO) with an untilISO between two item dates returns only items with published ≤ untilISO (and > sinceISO); inclusive-endpoint assertion (an item exactly at untilISO is included). Run node scripts/fetch-feed.js --selftest → FAIL (arity/behavior).
  2. GREEN. Extend windowItems(items, sinceISO, max, untilISO) (:87): after the lower-bound filter add if (untilISO) out = out.filter((it) => !it.published || it.published <= untilISO). Parse --until in the CLI arm (:152) via arg('--until', null) and pass it into windowItems (:160).
  3. GREEN — thread through fetchOne. In magazine-run.js change fetchOne(feed, since, max) → fetchOne(feed, since, max, until) and if (until) args.push('--until', until) (:94-103); update the cmdDiscover call site to pass until from T1's resolveWindow.
  4. Inline verification. Both selftests PASS. Proof-of-life: node scripts/fetch-feed.js --file tests/fixtures/<feed>.xml --since 2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z --until 2021-01-01T00:00:00.000Z → JSON array contains only items published in 2020 (assert by eyeballing/`jq` the published fields). An item dated exactly at the --until instant is present.

Depends on: T1.

T3: Date aliases + inclusive bare-date parsing

Slice shape: vertical — --from/--to YYYY-MM-DD resolve to correct inclusive ISO bounds.

Spec refs: FR-1.1, FR-6.2 · ACs: AC1, AC2 (parsing/semantics documented + selftested).

  1. RED. In magazine-run.js selftest: resolveWindow({from:'2026-03-01', to:'2026-03-31'}, now) → since === '2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z', until === '2026-03-31T23:59:59.999Z'; resolveWindow({since:'2026-03-01T12:00:00.000Z'}, now) passes the ISO through verbatim; a malformed date (--from notadate) throws a clear error. Run → FAIL.
  2. GREEN. In resolveWindow: normalize --from via new Date(v+'T00:00:00.000Z'), --to via end-of-day (+'T23:59:59.999Z'); --since/--until pass through but validate with new Date(v) (NaN → throw naming the flag). Bare-date regex /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ distinguishes alias from ISO.
  3. Inline verification. selftest PASS. Proof-of-life: node scripts/magazine-run.js discover --feed tests/fixtures/<feed>.xml --from 2020-01-01 --to 2020-12-31 exits 0 and the snapshot contains only 2020 items.

Depends on: T2.

T4: Persist single-latest discover snapshot

Slice shape: vertical — a discover run records its item set as the one durable snapshot.

Spec refs: FR-2.1, D2 · ACs: AC5.

  1. RED. selftest: after cmdDiscover against a fixture, state.load(file).snapshot exists with {id: <ISO>, guids: [<the snapshot guids>]}; a second cmdDiscover overwrites it (id changes, no list accumulates — state.load(file).snapshot is an object, not an array of snapshots). Run → FAIL.
  2. GREEN. In cmdDiscover, before the final state.save (:143), set st.snapshot = { id: new Date().toISOString(), guids: snapshot.map((s) => s.guid) }. The printed return value (snapshot array) is unchanged. Snapshot id is the discover timestamp (pass nowISO in so the selftest is deterministic).
  3. Inline verification. selftest PASS. Proof-of-life: run discover twice on a fixture; cat <root>/state.json | jq '.snapshot | {id, n: (.guids|length)}' shows one object whose id changed between runs and whose guids count matches the printed array length.

Depends on: T1.

T5: Scope prep to the latest snapshot (+ D6 reuse semantics)

Slice shape: vertical — prep crawls only the snapshot's discovered items; a ledger full of stale discovered items can't balloon it.

Spec refs: FR-2.2, FR-2.3, D2, D6 · ACs: AC6, AC7.

  1. RED. selftest: seed a state with 3 discovered items where only 2 are in state.snapshot.guids; run cmdPrep with an injected/stubbed crawler; assert the out-of-snapshot item is not crawled (stays discovered) while the 2 in-snapshot items advance. Add a D6 case: a snapshot guid already at summarized is not re-crawled (skipped by the existing status filter) yet remains referenced by the snapshot. Absent-snapshot case: with no state.snapshot, prep falls back to the current whole-ledger behavior (Inv-4). Run → FAIL.
  2. GREEN. In cmdPrep (:166), compute const scope = st.snapshot && Array.isArray(st.snapshot.guids) ? new Set(st.snapshot.guids) : null. Change the loop guard to: if (scope && !scope.has(guid)) continue; placed before the existing if (item.status !== 'discovered') continue;. (--snapshot <id> is parsed but ignored — single-snapshot store; leave a one-line comment marking it the inert hook.)
  3. Inline verification. selftest PASS. Proof-of-life: seed a fixture root with 1 in-snapshot + 1 out-of-snapshot discovered item, run node scripts/magazine-run.js prep --root <root>, assert the printed route.discovered counts only the in-snapshot item and the out-of-snapshot item's status in state.json is still discovered.

Depends on: T4.

T6: Make the cursor watch-internal (stop interactive cursor advance)

Slice shape: refactor-prep — removes the interactive path's dependence on / advancement of the per-feed cursor (P1); no new user-observable behavior, but it is the load-bearing D7/AC8 change and must not regress watch.

Spec refs: D7, Inv-4, Risks (cursor) · ACs: AC8.

  1. Study. grep -n "advanceCursors\|cursors\[" scripts/*.js reference/pipeline.md to enumerate every cursor writer. Confirm: cmdEnqueue (watch producer) advances its own forward-only cursor — leave untouched; the interactive Phase-6 render advance (pipeline.md:107 instruction + any advanceCursors call reachable only from the interactive build) is the only one to remove.
  2. RED/guard. Confirm cmdDiscover no longer reads st.cursors for the lower bound (already true after T1 — add an explicit selftest assertion that resolveWindow never consults cursors). Add a selftest asserting cmdEnqueue still advances its cursor (or rely on the existing FR-R4 enqueue selftest — cite it).
  3. GREEN. Remove the interactive cursor-advance (the Phase-6 advanceCursors() instruction and any interactive-only call); keep the function + the enqueue caller. If advanceCursors is shared, gate it so only enqueue/drain invoke it.
  4. Inline verification. bash tests/watch.test.sh → all pass (enqueue/drain + FR-7/FR-R4 cursor invariants intact). bash tests/structure.test.sh → pass. Behavioral: re-run discover twice with the same window on a fixture; assert state.json.cursors is unchanged by the interactive runs (only enqueue mutates it).

Depends on: T1.

T7: Rewrite the windowing docs to the real flag surface

Slice shape: config — documentation only; no end-to-end behavior, but AC4 mandates the doc/entrypoint mismatch is closed.

Spec refs: FR-1.3, FR-1.4, D7 · ACs: AC4.

  1. SKILL.md Phase 2 (:150-160): replace the discover [--since <cursor>] [--max <N>] signature with the real surface discover (--from <YYYY-MM-DD> --to <YYYY-MM-DD> | --since <ISO> --until <ISO> | --days N) [--max <N>]; state that a window is required and bare discover errors; drop any "compute --since from --days by hand" language; note the cursor is watch-internal only.
  2. argument-hint (:5): ensure --days N stays and add the window flags context for builds; do not advertise --since (deprecated/hidden) in the hint.
  3. reference/pipeline.md §Windowing (:14-24, :43, :53, :107): rewrite the 3-tier "lookback → cursor since-last-run" model to "explicit window required (from/to or days); the cursor is the scheduler's (watch) forward-only poll memory, not an interactive default"; update the example discover line; remove the Phase-6 "call advanceCursors() so the next since-last-run starts cleanly" interactive instruction (now watch-only).
  4. Inline verification. bash tests/structure.test.sh → pass (SKILL.md frontmatter/section gates green). grep -n "since last run\|--since <cursor>" SKILL.md reference/pipeline.md → no interactive-default cursor language remains. Manual read: Phase 2 signature matches the flags T1–T3 actually parse.

Depends on: T1, T2, T3, T6.

T8: Dogfood / Utility Verification (TN−1)

Slice shape: vertical — exercises the real deliverable on a representative task.

Spec refs: FR-1.x/2.x/6.x (whole story) · Archetype: CLI real-invocation + golden spot-check.

Scenario: Using the bundled test fixtures as feeds, drive the real Stage-A flow the way the skill does: (1) discover with a deliberately narrow range --from/--to that should select a known subset of fixture items; (2) discover with a wider, overlapping range (the monthly-over-weekly case) and confirm already-discovered items keep their status (D6) and the snapshot is overwritten to the wider set; (3) prep and confirm it crawls only the latest snapshot; (4) bare discover and an unknown flag both exit 64 with a clear message.

Objective criteria (exact): a script tests/fixtures/dogfood_xck.sh (or inline commands) asserts: narrow-range snapshot guid set ⊂ wide-range snapshot guid set; state.json.snapshot is a single object after both runs; an out-of-snapshot discovered item is left uncrawled by prep; bare-discover exit code == 64; bash tests/structure.test.sh exits 0; bash tests/watch.test.sh exits 0.

Subjective rubric (blind judge): dimensions — error-message clarity (does the bare/unknown-flag message tell a user exactly what to pass?), doc-truthfulness (does SKILL.md Phase 2 match observed CLI behavior?), reuse-intuitiveness (is the overlapping-window reuse behavior unsurprising?). Satisfied bar: ≥4/5 on each, no dimension below 4.

Judge: a fresh blind subagent given only the design_doc + the dogfood transcript (not this plan), per _shared/dogfooding.md#judge. Emits a one-line verdict DOGFOOD: SHIP | ITERATE that /verify reads.

Depends on: T5, T6, T7.

T9: Final verification (TN)

Slice shape: config — verification rollup.

  1. node scripts/magazine-run.js --selftest → PASS; node scripts/fetch-feed.js --selftest → PASS.
  2. bash tests/structure.test.sh → exit 0 (runs every script selftest + SKILL.md gates); bash tests/watch.test.sh → exit 0; bash tests/bundles.test.sh → exit 0.
  3. Force the error paths: bare discover → 64; discover --nope → 64 naming the flag; discover --from baddate → throws a clear date error.
  4. Skill conformance walkthrough (AC9): confirm no version-bump / changelog / README / manifest-sync change is present in the diff; canonical skill path unchanged; skill-patterns.md §A–§L spot-check (argument-hint matches parsed flags; no hard-coded user paths).
  5. Done-when walkthrough: narrate one real build window (e.g. --days 7) from discover (window resolved, snapshot persisted) → prep (only snapshot crawled) → confirm cursor untouched interactively and watch.test.sh green — tracing the Done-when line end to end.
  6. Cleanup: remove any temp fixture roots created under the system temp dir; no debug logging left in the scripts.

Depends on: T8.

Execution order & risks

Order: T1 → (T2 → T3) and T4 in parallel after T1 [P] → T5 (after T4) → T6 (after T1) → T7 (after T1,T2,T3,T6) → T8 → T9. Single implicit phase (9 tasks ≤ 12 → no ## Phase N groupings).

RiskSeverityMitigation (task)
Removing interactive cursor advance regresses watch's scheduled enqueue.MedT6 leaves cmdEnqueue untouched and gates watch.test.sh green as its done-when; T9 re-runs it.
Loud-reject breaks a verb that legitimately passes a now-"unknown" flag.LowAudited at grill (all caller flags known); T1 registers window flags in the shared bag; T9 runs bundles.test.sh (watch wrapper path).
Bare-date endpoint off-by-one (exclusive vs inclusive).LowT3 pins inclusive endpoints (--to → end-of-day) with an explicit selftest at the boundary instant.

Open questions: none — D1/D2/D6/D7 + AC8 (resolved as P1) close every fork.

pmos Source: docs/pmos/features/2026-06-24_magazine-pipeline-robustness/stories/260624-xck/03_plan.html · Created using pmos‑learnkit
